taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Software Engineering, General Software Engineer
core_skills: Object-oriented programming, Data structures, AI coding assistants, Large language models (LLMs), Code refactoring
skill_match_scores: Object-oriented programming:1.0, Data structures:1.0, AI coding assistants:0.5, Large language models (LLMs):1.0, Code refactoring:1.0
must_have_quals: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field, 0-2 years of professional software development experience (internships, co-ops, or relevant academic projects will be considered), Solid understanding of object-oriented programming (OOP) principles and data structures, Demonstrated experience using AI coding assistants (e.g., GitHub Copilot, Cursor) to accelerate code generation, automate boilerplate reduction, and streamline debugging workflows, Proven ability to leverage LLMs to refactor legacy codebases, improve code readability, and generate comprehensive documentation and unit tests, Proficiency in at least one modern programming language (e.g., C#, Java, Python, JavaScript), Basic understanding of software development lifecycle (SDLC) and agile methodologies, Strong problem-solving skills and an analytical mindset, Excellent written and verbal communication skills, Ability to work effectively both independently and as part of a team
preferred_quals: Familiarity with web development concepts (e.g., HTML, CSS, JavaScript, front-end frameworks) and/or database technologies (e.g., SQL, NoSQL) is a plus, Prior experience with healthcare IT or a regulated industry is a bonus, but not required
core_responsibilities: Collaborate with senior engineers and cross-functional teams (Product, QA, UX) to understand requirements and design software solutions, Write clean, efficient, and well-documented code primarily in [mention specific languages, e.g., C#, Python, JavaScript] within an agile development environment, Participate in code reviews, providing and receiving constructive feedback to ensure code quality and adherence to best practices, Assist in identifying, diagnosing, and resolving software defects and performance issues, Contribute to the testing of software components, including unit, integration, and system testing, Learn and apply new technologies, tools, and methodologies relevant to software development and the healthcare industry, Actively participate in team meetings, stand-ups, and other collaborative activities, Adhere to company coding standards, security policies, and development processes
job_summary: ModMed is a company focused on modernizing the healthcare experience through innovative software solutions. They are seeking a highly motivated Software Engineer 1 to contribute to the development and enhancement of their healthcare technology, working under experienced engineers to deliver high-quality software that improves patient care.
